title: AP U.S. History MCQ Practice Exam 12

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation with described map. A historian argues that early Spanish contact in the Caribbean transformed Taino communities through disease, forced labor, missionary activity, tribute demands, and resistance. A described map shows Hispaniola, Spanish settlements near gold fields, coastal landing points, and Taino villages disrupted by new labor demands. Which evidence would best support this interpretation?

choices.A: Archaeological and Spanish administrative records showing population decline, tribute collection, and forced labor near mining settlements
choices.B: Federal reports describing railroad time zones in the 1880s
choices.C: Newspaper accounts of the Scopes Trial
choices.D: A table of post-2008 mortgage foreclosures

questions.correctAnswerText: Archaeological and Spanish administrative records showing population decline, tribute collection, and forced labor near mining settlements
questions.explanation: These records directly support the interpretation of demographic disruption, tribute, mining, and coerced labor in the early Spanish Caribbean.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation with described map. A historian argues that early Spanish contact in the Caribbean transformed Taino communities through disease, forced labor, missionary activity, tribute demands, and resistance. Which broader context best explains these changes?

choices.A: British colonists had already created elected assemblies throughout the Caribbean.
choices.B: European conquest connected Native societies to Atlantic empires, Christianity, coerced labor, and demographic upheaval.
choices.C: Taino communities controlled Spanish imperial administration after 1492.
choices.D: Spanish colonization had no relationship to labor extraction.

questions.correctAnswerText: European conquest connected Native societies to Atlantic empires, Christianity, coerced labor, and demographic upheaval.
questions.explanation: The source describes early conquest as a process involving empire, missionary work, forced labor, disease, and Native disruption.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation with described map. A historian argues that early Spanish contact in the Caribbean transformed Taino communities through disease, forced labor, missionary activity, tribute demands, and resistance. Which argument about early European colonization is best supported?

choices.A: Spanish colonization in the Caribbean involved both violent disruption and Native attempts to respond to new imperial pressures.
choices.B: Spanish contact left Caribbean Native societies unchanged.
choices.C: European colonization in the Caribbean depended only on voluntary trade.
choices.D: Native resistance prevented all Spanish settlement in the Caribbean.

questions.correctAnswerText: Spanish colonization in the Caribbean involved both violent disruption and Native attempts to respond to new imperial pressures.
questions.explanation: The interpretation emphasizes disease, forced labor, tribute, missionary pressure, and resistance.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated South Carolina council report, 1715, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “Our former trading partners accuse the colony of debt, fraud, enslavement, and insult. The frontier posts are in danger, and merchants fear the collapse of the deerskin trade.” Which conflict is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The Nullification Crisis
choices.B: The Yamasee War and conflict over trade, debt, and colonial expansion
choices.C: The Whiskey Rebellion
choices.D: The Mexican-American War

questions.correctAnswerText: The Yamasee War and conflict over trade, debt, and colonial expansion
questions.explanation: The report’s references to Native trading partners, debt, fraud, enslavement, and frontier danger point to the Yamasee War.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated South Carolina council report, 1715, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The report says Native trading partners accused the colony of debt, fraud, enslavement, and insult, while merchants feared collapse of the deerskin trade. Which claim is best supported?

choices.A: Native-colonial trade relationships could become sources of conflict as well as cooperation.
choices.B: South Carolina’s economy had no connection to Native trade.
choices.C: Native peoples had no role in shaping frontier politics.
choices.D: Colonial trade eliminated all violence between Native peoples and settlers.

questions.correctAnswerText: Native-colonial trade relationships could become sources of conflict as well as cooperation.
questions.explanation: The source shows trade creating dependency, grievances, and violence rather than only peaceful exchange.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated South Carolina council report, 1715, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The report describes Native anger over debt, fraud, enslavement, insult, and colonial frontier posts. Which broader colonial context best explains the conflict?

choices.A: Expansion of English settlement, trade dependency, and Native resistance in the Southeast
choices.B: Decline of all settlement in the Carolina backcountry
choices.C: Federal land surveys under the Northwest Ordinance
choices.D: The end of Atlantic trade before 1700

questions.correctAnswerText: Expansion of English settlement, trade dependency, and Native resistance in the Southeast
questions.explanation: The Yamasee War grew from colonial expansion, trade abuses, enslavement, and Native resistance.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated South Carolina council report, 1715, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The report links Native grievances, frontier danger, trade, debt, and enslavement. Which argument about colonial borderlands is best supported?

choices.A: Borderlands were shaped only by European diplomacy.
choices.B: Native peoples and colonists negotiated, traded, and fought within unstable systems of power and exchange.
choices.C: Colonial frontier trade prevented all Native resistance.
choices.D: The Southeast was isolated from Atlantic markets.

questions.correctAnswerText: Native peoples and colonists negotiated, traded, and fought within unstable systems of power and exchange.
questions.explanation: The report shows borderlands as contested spaces involving commerce, power, and violence.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of colonial cooperation during the Seven Years’ War era.
Pattern | Development
Albany Congress | Proposed greater colonial coordination
British war goals | Defeat France and secure interior territory
Colonial assemblies | Often protected local control over taxation and militia
Native diplomacy | Remained crucial in the interior
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The war ended all conflict between colonies and Britain.
choices.B: The Albany Congress created a permanent colonial union.
choices.C: Imperial war exposed both the need for coordination and the persistence of local colonial interests.
choices.D: Native diplomacy became irrelevant during the war.

questions.correctAnswerText: Imperial war exposed both the need for coordination and the persistence of local colonial interests.
questions.explanation: The table shows coordination efforts alongside assembly resistance and local interests.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of colonial cooperation during the Seven Years’ War era. The table notes the Albany Congress, British war goals against France, colonial assemblies protecting local control, and the importance of Native diplomacy. Which broader context best explains the developments shown?

choices.A: Competition among British, French, and Native powers over the North American interior
choices.B: Federal efforts to desegregate public schools
choices.C: Debates over income taxes during the Progressive Era
choices.D: The rise of biotechnology after 1980

questions.correctAnswerText: Competition among British, French, and Native powers over the North American interior
questions.explanation: The Seven Years’ War grew from imperial rivalry and Native diplomacy in the interior.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of colonial cooperation during the Seven Years’ War era. The Albany Congress proposed greater coordination, British officials pursued war goals, colonial assemblies guarded local control, and Native diplomacy remained crucial. Which later development was influenced by tensions revealed during and after the war?

choices.A: The imperial crisis over taxation, authority, and colonial rights
choices.B: The collapse of all colonial assemblies
choices.C: The immediate creation of the Constitution in 1763
choices.D: The end of British authority in Canada

questions.correctAnswerText: The imperial crisis over taxation, authority, and colonial rights
questions.explanation: Postwar imperial reform and colonial resistance followed tensions exposed and intensified by the war.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of colonial cooperation during the Seven Years’ War era. The table shows proposed colonial coordination, British imperial goals, local assembly resistance, and Native diplomacy. Which argument is best supported?

choices.A: The war strengthened empire while also revealing political disagreements that later contributed to imperial crisis.
choices.B: The war made colonial self-government disappear completely.
choices.C: The war eliminated Native influence in the interior.
choices.D: The war reduced British interest in North America.

questions.correctAnswerText: The war strengthened empire while also revealing political disagreements that later contributed to imperial crisis.
questions.explanation: British victory expanded imperial power but increased disputes over authority and colonial obligations.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Virginia religious freedom petition, 1780s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “No citizen’s tax should build another man’s church, and no office should test the soul. Religion, if true, needs no magistrate to compel belief.” Which issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The campaign for temperance reform
choices.B: Disestablishment and religious liberty in the Revolutionary era
choices.C: The debate over the Kansas-Nebraska Act
choices.D: The creation of the Freedmen’s Bureau

questions.correctAnswerText: Disestablishment and religious liberty in the Revolutionary era
questions.explanation: The petition’s opposition to tax-supported churches and religious tests reflects disestablishment.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Virginia religious freedom petition, 1780s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The petition opposes tax support for churches and religious tests for office. Which claim is best supported?

choices.A: Revolutionary-era ideas of liberty could extend to church-state relations and freedom of conscience.
choices.B: State governments universally strengthened established churches after independence.
choices.C: Religious liberty debates had no relationship to republican government.
choices.D: Petitioners wanted Congress to create one national church.

questions.correctAnswerText: Revolutionary-era ideas of liberty could extend to church-state relations and freedom of conscience.
questions.explanation: The source applies liberty to religion, taxes, and public office.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Virginia religious freedom petition, 1780s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The petition rejects tax-supported churches and religious tests for public office. Which later principle is most closely connected to this argument?

choices.A: Federal protection for free exercise and limits on religious establishment
choices.B: Federal support for plantation slavery
choices.C: National prohibition of immigration
choices.D: Federal creation of standard time zones

questions.correctAnswerText: Federal protection for free exercise and limits on religious establishment
questions.explanation: The petition anticipates constitutional principles limiting establishment and protecting religious liberty.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated diplomatic summary, 1823, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “European monarchies must not treat the newly independent republics of this hemisphere as fields for recolonization. The United States claims no right to govern them, but it declares a separate political interest in the Americas.” Which policy is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The Open Door policy
choices.B: Containment
choices.C: The Monroe Doctrine
choices.D: The Teller Amendment

questions.correctAnswerText: The Monroe Doctrine
questions.explanation: The warning against European recolonization and claim of a U.S. interest in the Americas reflect the Monroe Doctrine.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated diplomatic summary, 1823, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The summary warns European monarchies against recolonizing newly independent republics in the Americas. Which broader context best explains the policy?

choices.A: Latin American independence movements and U.S. concern over European intervention
choices.B: The rise of the New Deal
choices.C: The creation of NATO after World War II
choices.D: The end of all Spanish influence in North America before 1700

questions.correctAnswerText: Latin American independence movements and U.S. concern over European intervention
questions.explanation: The Monroe Doctrine responded to new Latin American republics and fear of European recolonization.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated diplomatic summary, 1823, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The statement declares a separate U.S. political interest in the Americas while denying a right to govern newly independent republics. The purpose of the statement was most likely to do which of the following?

choices.A: Invite European monarchies to recolonize Latin America
choices.B: Announce that the United States would abandon the Western Hemisphere
choices.C: Warn European powers while defining a U.S. sphere of interest
choices.D: Create immediate U.S. colonies in every Latin American republic

questions.correctAnswerText: Warn European powers while defining a U.S. sphere of interest
questions.explanation: The statement warns Europe while asserting a distinct U.S. interest in the hemisphere.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated diplomatic summary, 1823, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The statement warns against European recolonization and declares a separate U.S. political interest in the Americas. Which argument about early nineteenth-century foreign policy is best supported?

choices.A: The United States sought to define regional influence even while its military power remained limited.
choices.B: The United States rejected all interest in the Western Hemisphere.
choices.C: The Monroe Doctrine created an alliance with European monarchies.
choices.D: Latin American independence had no effect on U.S. policy.

questions.correctAnswerText: The United States sought to define regional influence even while its military power remained limited.
questions.explanation: The doctrine asserted hemispheric claims before the United States had overwhelming military capacity.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated free Black church meeting notice, 1830s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “Members will gather to raise school funds, support widows, aid fugitives when law permits, and defend the dignity of our people against insult in streets, courts, and churches.” Which development is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Free Black institution-building in northern communities
choices.B: The creation of the Social Security system
choices.C: The rise of the cattle kingdom
choices.D: The annexation of the Philippines

questions.correctAnswerText: Free Black institution-building in northern communities
questions.explanation: The notice shows Black churches supporting schools, mutual aid, fugitives, and public dignity.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated free Black church meeting notice, 1830s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The notice calls for school funds, support for widows, aid to fugitives when law permits, and defense against insult in streets, courts, and churches. Which claim is best supported?

choices.A: Free Black churches and mutual-aid networks combined religion, education, community welfare, and civil rights advocacy.
choices.B: Free Black communities avoided institution-building before the Civil War.
choices.C: Northern free Black activism focused only on western land policy.
choices.D: Free Black churches rejected education and community support.

questions.correctAnswerText: Free Black churches and mutual-aid networks combined religion, education, community welfare, and civil rights advocacy.
questions.explanation: The notice links church life to schooling, welfare, fugitives, and resistance to discrimination.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated free Black church meeting notice, 1830s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The notice links schooling, mutual aid, fugitives, and dignity against discrimination. Which broader antebellum issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The struggle by free African Americans to claim citizenship, community autonomy, and public respect despite racism
choices.B: The decline of all Black activism before emancipation
choices.C: The federal government’s full protection of free Black rights
choices.D: The end of racial discrimination in northern cities

questions.correctAnswerText: The struggle by free African Americans to claim citizenship, community autonomy, and public respect despite racism
questions.explanation: The source shows free Black communities organizing for dignity and rights despite discrimination.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of Pacific Railway Act effects.
Policy or condition | Development
Federal land grants | Supported railroad construction
War context | Southern opposition weakened during the Civil War
Railroad route | Linked eastern networks to the Pacific
Labor force | Included Irish, Chinese, Civil War veteran, and other workers
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The Civil War era federal government promoted western economic integration through railroad construction.
choices.B: The Pacific Railway Act prevented settlement beyond the Mississippi River.
choices.C: Transcontinental railroads were built without federal assistance.
choices.D: Railroad construction relied only on enslaved labor.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Civil War era federal government promoted western economic integration through railroad construction.
questions.explanation: The table links federal land grants, wartime politics, labor, and transcontinental connection.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of Pacific Railway Act effects. Federal land grants supported railroad construction, Civil War politics weakened southern opposition, eastern networks were linked to the Pacific, and the labor force included Irish, Chinese, Civil War veteran, and other workers. Which geographic effect was most directly connected to this development?

choices.A: Faster movement of people, goods, and information across the continent
choices.B: Complete isolation of Pacific communities from eastern markets
choices.C: End of all migration into western territories
choices.D: Elimination of railroad towns in the Great Plains

questions.correctAnswerText: Faster movement of people, goods, and information across the continent
questions.explanation: A transcontinental railroad directly accelerated continental movement and market integration.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of Pacific Railway Act effects. The table notes federal land grants, Civil War context, a route linking eastern networks to the Pacific, and a diverse railroad labor force. Which broader context best explains the act?

choices.A: Federal withdrawal from economic development during the Civil War
choices.B: Wartime Republican support for free-labor development, western settlement, and national market integration
choices.C: Congressional opposition to all transportation improvement
choices.D: British control of U.S. western railroads after 1861

questions.correctAnswerText: Wartime Republican support for free-labor development, western settlement, and national market integration
questions.explanation: The act reflected Republican development goals made easier by the absence of southern opposition.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of Pacific Railway Act effects. Federal land grants supported railroad construction, the Civil War changed congressional politics, eastern networks linked to the Pacific, and a diverse labor force built the railroad. Which argument about the Civil War era is best supported?

choices.A: The federal government expanded its role in economic development even while fighting the war.
choices.B: The Civil War stopped all national infrastructure projects.
choices.C: Western railroads had no relationship to national identity.
choices.D: Railroad policy ended all sectional and racial conflict.

questions.correctAnswerText: The federal government expanded its role in economic development even while fighting the war.
questions.explanation: The Pacific Railway Act shows expanded federal economic action during the Civil War.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Reconstruction congressional speech, 1868, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “If a president may dismiss the officers charged with carrying out the laws, and then defeat Congress by refusing execution, the nation must ask whether Reconstruction belongs to one man or to the people’s representatives.” Which issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The impeachment crisis involving Andrew Johnson and congressional Reconstruction
choices.B: The election of 1800
choices.C: The passage of the National Origins Act
choices.D: The Iran-Contra hearings

questions.correctAnswerText: The impeachment crisis involving Andrew Johnson and congressional Reconstruction
questions.explanation: The speech refers to presidential resistance to congressional Reconstruction and dismissal of officials.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Reconstruction congressional speech, 1868, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The speaker argues that presidential dismissal of officers and refusal to execute laws raises whether Reconstruction belongs to the president or Congress. Which claim is best supported?

choices.A: Reconstruction involved conflict over separation of powers as well as policy toward the South.
choices.B: Congress and the president agreed on every Reconstruction policy.
choices.C: The impeachment crisis concerned only foreign policy.
choices.D: Presidential power disappeared during Reconstruction.

questions.correctAnswerText: Reconstruction involved conflict over separation of powers as well as policy toward the South.
questions.explanation: The source frames Reconstruction as a struggle between Congress and the president.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Reconstruction congressional speech, 1868, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The speech asks whether Reconstruction belongs to “one man” or “the people’s representatives.” Which broader context best explains the conflict?

choices.A: Disputes over how to readmit southern states and protect freedpeople’s rights after the Civil War
choices.B: The creation of Spanish missions in California
choices.C: The rise of Social Darwinism in the 1880s
choices.D: U.S. entry into NATO

questions.correctAnswerText: Disputes over how to readmit southern states and protect freedpeople’s rights after the Civil War
questions.explanation: Johnson’s conflict with Congress centered on the direction of Reconstruction.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Reconstruction congressional speech, 1868, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The speech criticizes presidential resistance to congressional Reconstruction laws. Which development was most directly connected to the congressional position?

choices.A: Military Reconstruction Acts and federal efforts to protect Black civil and political rights
choices.B: The Missouri Compromise
choices.C: The Alien and Sedition Acts
choices.D: The Open Door policy

questions.correctAnswerText: Military Reconstruction Acts and federal efforts to protect Black civil and political rights
questions.explanation: Congressional Reconstruction involved military districts and stronger protections for freedpeople’s rights.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1894. Hundreds of unemployed men march toward Washington, D.C., carrying signs demanding public works and relief during an economic depression. A leader speaks from a wagon about “the right to work.” Which event is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Coxey’s Army and protest during the depression of the 1890s
choices.B: The Boston Tea Party
choices.C: Freedom Summer
choices.D: The Oregon boundary settlement

questions.correctAnswerText: Coxey’s Army and protest during the depression of the 1890s
questions.explanation: The march on Washington by unemployed men demanding public works reflects Coxey’s Army.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1894. Unemployed men march toward Washington, D.C., carrying signs demanding public works and relief during an economic depression. Which broader economic context best explains the protest?

choices.A: The Panic of 1893, unemployment, and demands for federal action during industrial depression
choices.B: The rise of Cold War consumer culture
choices.C: The end of all wage labor in cities
choices.D: The creation of the Northwest Ordinance

questions.correctAnswerText: The Panic of 1893, unemployment, and demands for federal action during industrial depression
questions.explanation: Coxey’s Army emerged from severe unemployment and economic distress after the Panic of 1893.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1894. Unemployed workers march toward Washington, D.C., demanding public works and relief. The marchers’ likely purpose was to do which of the following?

choices.A: Persuade the federal government to address unemployment and economic distress
choices.B: Demand restoration of British colonial rule
choices.C: Oppose all public works programs
choices.D: Defend corporate wage cuts as natural law

questions.correctAnswerText: Persuade the federal government to address unemployment and economic distress
questions.explanation: The marchers demanded public works and relief from the federal government.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1894. Unemployed workers march on Washington demanding public works and relief during economic depression. Which argument about the Gilded Age is best supported?

choices.A: Industrial depression encouraged some Americans to demand a larger federal role in economic security.
choices.B: The federal government had already created a modern welfare state by 1894.
choices.C: Unemployment had no political consequences in the 1890s.
choices.D: Industrial workers avoided public protest.

questions.correctAnswerText: Industrial depression encouraged some Americans to demand a larger federal role in economic security.
questions.explanation: The protest shows pressure for federal action before the modern welfare state.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated anti-lynching pamphlet, 1890s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “The mob calls itself justice, yet it silences witnesses, mocks the courts, and teaches every Black family that law may vanish at the rope’s end.” Which movement is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Anti-lynching activism and Black journalism against racial terror
choices.B: The Free Soil movement
choices.C: The New Right
choices.D: The campaign for Lend-Lease

questions.correctAnswerText: Anti-lynching activism and Black journalism against racial terror
questions.explanation: The pamphlet’s criticism of mobs and failed courts reflects anti-lynching activism.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated anti-lynching pamphlet, 1890s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The pamphlet says mobs call themselves justice but silence witnesses, mock courts, and threaten Black families. Which claim is best supported?

choices.A: Anti-lynching activists argued that racial violence undermined both Black rights and the rule of law.
choices.B: Lynching was fully prosecuted by southern courts in the 1890s.
choices.C: Black journalists avoided criticism of racial violence.
choices.D: Anti-lynching activism had no relationship to citizenship.

questions.correctAnswerText: Anti-lynching activists argued that racial violence undermined both Black rights and the rule of law.
questions.explanation: The source condemns mob violence as a denial of law and citizenship.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated anti-lynching pamphlet, 1890s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The pamphlet condemns mobs, failed courts, racial terror, and the denial of legal protection to Black families. Which broader pattern is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Black activists continued to challenge racial violence and unequal citizenship after Reconstruction.
choices.B: The federal government eliminated all racial violence after 1877.
choices.C: Southern states fully protected Black voting and legal rights in the 1890s.
choices.D: Racial violence declined because segregation ended.

questions.correctAnswerText: Black activists continued to challenge racial violence and unequal citizenship after Reconstruction.
questions.explanation: The pamphlet shows continued Black activism against violence and legal inequality in the 1890s.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Anti-Imperialist League statement, 1899, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “A republic born from consent cannot hold islands by force and call it liberty. Empire abroad will corrupt freedom at home.” Which issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Opposition to U.S. annexation and rule of the Philippines after the Spanish-American War
choices.B: Support for the Monroe Doctrine in 1823
choices.C: Opposition to the Northwest Ordinance
choices.D: Debate over the Whiskey Rebellion

questions.correctAnswerText: Opposition to U.S. annexation and rule of the Philippines after the Spanish-American War
questions.explanation: The source criticizes holding islands by force and reflects anti-imperial opposition.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Anti-Imperialist League statement, 1899, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The statement argues that a republic based on consent cannot hold islands by force. Which point of view is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Anti-imperialists used founding ideals to criticize overseas empire.
choices.B: Anti-imperialists demanded permanent colonial rule over the Philippines.
choices.C: Anti-imperialists rejected the language of republican liberty.
choices.D: Anti-imperialists supported monarchy as a better system than republican government.

questions.correctAnswerText: Anti-imperialists used founding ideals to criticize overseas empire.
questions.explanation: The statement uses consent and republican liberty to criticize colonial rule.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Anti-Imperialist League statement, 1899, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The statement condemns holding islands by force and warns that empire abroad corrupts freedom at home. Which broader context best explains this argument?

choices.A: U.S. victory over Spain raised debates about whether the United States should become an overseas imperial power.
choices.B: The United States had renounced all Pacific influence before 1898.
choices.C: The Civil War ended all debates over republican government.
choices.D: The Philippines had been part of the United States since the American Revolution.

questions.correctAnswerText: U.S. victory over Spain raised debates about whether the United States should become an overseas imperial power.
questions.explanation: The Spanish-American War and Philippine annexation triggered anti-imperialist arguments.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Anti-Imperialist League statement, 1899, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The statement argues that empire abroad threatens liberty at home. Which argument about U.S. imperialism is best supported?

choices.A: U.S. expansion overseas generated debate over whether republican ideals could coexist with colonial rule.
choices.B: All Americans agreed that overseas empire strengthened democracy.
choices.C: Anti-imperialists avoided moral and constitutional arguments.
choices.D: Overseas imperialism had no relationship to national identity.

questions.correctAnswerText: U.S. expansion overseas generated debate over whether republican ideals could coexist with colonial rule.
questions.explanation: The source argues that empire abroad threatens republican liberty at home.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Described map and table of Tennessee Valley Authority projects, 1930s.
Feature | Development
Dams | Built for flood control and power
Rural electricity | Expanded in affected counties
Soil conservation | Promoted in farming areas
Local employment | Increased through construction projects
Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.A: The New Deal used regional planning to address poverty, energy access, flooding, and environmental management.
choices.B: The TVA ended all federal involvement in regional development.
choices.C: New Deal programs avoided rural areas.
choices.D: Rural electrification declined because of the TVA.

questions.correctAnswerText: The New Deal used regional planning to address poverty, energy access, flooding, and environmental management.
questions.explanation: TVA dams, electricity, soil conservation, and jobs show federal regional planning.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map and table of Tennessee Valley Authority projects, 1930s. Dams were built for flood control and power, rural electricity expanded, soil conservation was promoted, and local employment increased through construction. Which broader context best explains the program?

choices.A: New Deal experimentation with federal planning during the Great Depression
choices.B: The decline of federal power after 1933
choices.C: The disappearance of poverty in the rural South before the Depression
choices.D: The expansion of Spanish missions in California

questions.correctAnswerText: New Deal experimentation with federal planning during the Great Depression
questions.explanation: The TVA reflected New Deal experimentation in regional economic and environmental planning.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map and table of Tennessee Valley Authority projects, 1930s. The table shows dams, electricity, soil conservation, and local employment. Which debate was most closely connected to such programs?

choices.A: Whether the federal government should have a larger role in economic planning and regional development
choices.B: Whether Britain should tax colonial paper goods
choices.C: Whether churches should be established by state governments
choices.D: Whether railroads should adopt standard time zones

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether the federal government should have a larger role in economic planning and regional development
questions.explanation: TVA projects raised debates over federal planning and public power.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map and table of Tennessee Valley Authority projects, 1930s. Dams, rural electricity, soil conservation, and construction employment are shown. Which social effect was most directly connected to the program?

choices.A: Improved access to electricity and jobs for some rural communities
choices.B: Immediate end of all rural poverty
choices.C: Elimination of all regional inequality in the South
choices.D: Complete privatization of flood control

questions.correctAnswerText: Improved access to electricity and jobs for some rural communities
questions.explanation: Rural electrification and construction employment affected communities in the Tennessee Valley.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described newspaper photograph, Los Angeles, 1965. The image shows burned storefronts, police lines, National Guard vehicles, and residents gathered near signs protesting unemployment, police mistreatment, and poor housing. Which issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Urban unrest connected to racial inequality, policing, jobs, and housing
choices.B: The First Great Awakening
choices.C: The Oregon boundary dispute
choices.D: The creation of the Federal Reserve

questions.correctAnswerText: Urban unrest connected to racial inequality, policing, jobs, and housing
questions.explanation: The photograph’s signs, police lines, National Guard vehicles, and damage point to urban unrest and structural inequality.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described newspaper photograph, Los Angeles, 1965. Burned storefronts, police lines, National Guard vehicles, and signs protesting unemployment, police mistreatment, and poor housing appear. Which broader context best explains the unrest?

choices.A: Persistent racial segregation, uneven urban opportunity, police conflict, and postwar metropolitan change
choices.B: Complete success in eliminating racial inequality by 1965
choices.C: Federal withdrawal from all urban policy after World War II
choices.D: Decline of all Black migration to western cities before 1940

questions.correctAnswerText: Persistent racial segregation, uneven urban opportunity, police conflict, and postwar metropolitan change
questions.explanation: Urban uprisings grew from inequality in housing, policing, employment, and metropolitan development.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described newspaper photograph, Los Angeles, 1965. The photograph shows burned storefronts, police lines, National Guard vehicles, and residents near signs about unemployment, police mistreatment, and poor housing. The likely purpose of publishing such an image was to do which of the following?

choices.A: Draw public attention to urban crisis and the limits of formal civil rights victories
choices.B: Promote the idea that all urban problems had been solved
choices.C: Encourage the end of all public discussion of policing
choices.D: Defend colonial taxation policies

questions.correctAnswerText: Draw public attention to urban crisis and the limits of formal civil rights victories
questions.explanation: The image highlights unresolved urban problems after major civil rights victories.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described newspaper photograph, Los Angeles, 1965. The image shows urban destruction, police lines, National Guard vehicles, and protest signs about unemployment, police mistreatment, and housing. Which argument about the civil rights era is best supported?

choices.A: Legal victories did not fully resolve structural inequalities in northern and western cities.
choices.B: Civil rights conflicts were limited to voting rights in the rural South.
choices.C: Urban unrest had no relationship to racial inequality.
choices.D: Federal civil rights law immediately eliminated conflicts over jobs and housing.

questions.correctAnswerText: Legal victories did not fully resolve structural inequalities in northern and western cities.
questions.explanation: The photograph shows continuing inequality outside the rural South and beyond formal legal segregation.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of détente-era foreign policy, 1970s.
Pattern | Development
U.S.-Soviet arms talks | Produced agreements limiting some nuclear weapons
U.S.-China relations | Opened through diplomacy
Trade and grain sales | Expanded in some areas
Criticism | Some argued détente ignored human rights or strengthened rivals
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Détente combined negotiation with continuing Cold War rivalry and domestic criticism.
choices.B: Détente ended the Cold War completely in the 1970s.
choices.C: U.S.-China relations remained completely frozen after 1972.
choices.D: Arms-control talks eliminated nuclear weapons.

questions.correctAnswerText: Détente combined negotiation with continuing Cold War rivalry and domestic criticism.
questions.explanation: The table shows arms talks, China diplomacy, trade, and criticism of détente.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of détente-era foreign policy, 1970s. U.S.-Soviet arms talks produced some limits, U.S.-China relations opened, trade expanded in some areas, and critics argued détente ignored human rights or strengthened rivals. Which policy shift is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Movement from direct confrontation toward negotiation and strategic flexibility with Communist powers
choices.B: Complete U.S. withdrawal from global affairs
choices.C: End of all military alliances
choices.D: Abandonment of anticommunism by every U.S. political group

questions.correctAnswerText: Movement from direct confrontation toward negotiation and strategic flexibility with Communist powers
questions.explanation: Détente involved negotiation with the Soviet Union and opening relations with China.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of détente-era foreign policy, 1970s. The table notes arms talks, opened relations with China, trade, and criticism over human rights and strategic concessions. Which broader issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Debate over whether diplomacy with rivals protected national security or compromised American values
choices.B: Debate over whether to create the Monroe Doctrine
choices.C: Conflict over Reconstruction amendments
choices.D: Opposition to the Erie Canal

questions.correctAnswerText: Debate over whether diplomacy with rivals protected national security or compromised American values
questions.explanation: Critics debated whether détente improved security or ignored human rights and strengthened rivals.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of housing and financial crisis patterns, c. 2007–2010.
Pattern | Development
Mortgage lending | Risky loans expanded before the crisis
Housing prices | Fell sharply in many regions
Foreclosures | Increased in many communities
Federal response | Included bank rescue measures, stimulus spending, and regulatory debates
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The housing and financial crisis connected private lending, household wealth, community stability, and federal policy.
choices.B: Housing prices rose evenly in every region during the crisis.
choices.C: Federal officials refused to respond to financial instability.
choices.D: Foreclosures had no effect on communities.

questions.correctAnswerText: The housing and financial crisis connected private lending, household wealth, community stability, and federal policy.
questions.explanation: The table links mortgages, falling housing prices, foreclosures, and federal response.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of housing and financial crisis patterns, c. 2007–2010. Risky mortgage lending expanded before the crisis, housing prices fell, foreclosures increased, and federal responses included bank rescue measures, stimulus spending, and regulatory debates. Which broader context best explains the crisis?

choices.A: Growth of complex financial markets, household debt, deregulation debates, and housing speculation
choices.B: Decline of all mortgage lending after 1980
choices.C: Federal prohibition of homeownership after 2000
choices.D: The end of global financial connections before the crisis

questions.correctAnswerText: Growth of complex financial markets, household debt, deregulation debates, and housing speculation
questions.explanation: The crisis emerged from financial complexity, mortgage risk, debt, and housing speculation.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of housing and financial crisis patterns, c. 2007–2010. The table shows risky lending, falling housing prices, foreclosures, bank rescue measures, stimulus spending, and regulatory debates. Which earlier pattern is most similar?

choices.A: Recurring debates over whether the federal government should intervene during major economic crises
choices.B: Colonial debates over church membership
choices.C: The creation of the Oregon boundary settlement
choices.D: The development of Pacific Northwest plank-house societies

questions.correctAnswerText: Recurring debates over whether the federal government should intervene during major economic crises
questions.explanation: The response to the crisis revived long-running debates over federal economic intervention.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of housing and financial crisis patterns, c. 2007–2010. Risky mortgage lending expanded, housing prices fell, foreclosures increased, and federal responses produced debates over rescue, stimulus, and regulation. Which argument about recent U.S. history is best supported?

choices.A: Economic crises have continued to raise questions about inequality, risk, regulation, and the proper role of government.
choices.B: Financial crises ended public debate over regulation.
choices.C: Housing markets had no relationship to household security.
choices.D: Federal economic policy became irrelevant after 1980.

questions.correctAnswerText: Economic crises have continued to raise questions about inequality, risk, regulation, and the proper role of government.
questions.explanation: The table shows crisis conditions and debates over rescue, stimulus, regulation, and inequality.
